$schema: Hand-edited. Read by update-status.mjs and merged into live-status.json under `summary.productionReadiness`. Render in live-status.html → renderProductionReadiness().
_updateWorkflow: When a stage's status changes, update the matching stage entry in this file.
Append a row to `history` with { date: 'YYYY-MM-DD', stage, from, to, note, commit?: 'sha or PR url' }.
Keep `baseline` IMMUTABLE — that field captures the pre-work state on 2026-05-24. Only `current` and `history` evolve.
Run `node docs/status/update-status.mjs` to push the change through the cron pipeline.
DO NOT delete history entries — the dashboard renders them as the audit trail.

tracks.name: Voice / Digital Assistant
tracks.description: Production-readiness for the conversational voice agent (Wave E + Wave D). Spans intent coverage, eval regression suite, PHI safety, telephony backends, pilot ops, and runtime monitoring.

stages.name: Stage 1 — Capability completeness
stages.rationale: Every conversational intent (book / cancel / reschedule / confirm / list / pay / verify-insurance / transfer / end) has an end-to-end implementation + STRIDE adversarial test.

baseline.summary: 12 intents shipped end-to-end. 741+ tests across packages/voice-agent; 74% line / 87% branch coverage. STRIDE adversarial cases (prompt injection, identity bypass, cross-tenant leakage) covered.

current.summary: Unchanged. 12 intents, 741+ tests, 74% / 87% coverage.

stages.name: Stage 2 — Eval harness
stages.rationale: Regression suite of scripted golden conversations + scorers. Lets us change prompts / intents / state machine without holding our breath. Runs on every PR.

baseline.summary: No `evals/` or `golden-transcripts/` directory existed. Only `improvement/transcript-reviewer.ts` ran post-call heuristics — no pre-deployment gate, no per-intent SLA tracking, no hallucination detector.

current.summary: @cernia/voice-agent-evals package shipped. 8 goldens covering book-appointment, lookup-practitioner, transfer-to-human, PHI gate + verification dance (3-turn DOB/2FA), red-flag chest-pain short-circuit, unsupported-language transfer, cost-ceiling trip. 3 scorers (intent-emission, agent-text expectations, PHI-leak regex). 97 ms wall clock. Wired into CI via `pnpm --filter @cernia/voice-agent-evals test`. SHIPPED 2026-05-25: .github/workflows/voice-agent-evals.yml runs the 8-golden suite per-commit on push to main + every PR touching packages/voice-agent/** or packages/voice-agent-evals/**. Workflow-dispatch trigger for manual runs. ~100ms wall clock, no API keys required (deterministic scripted-claude fixture). Catches model-prompt regressions, intent-router drift, PHI-leak regressions, red-flag screener failures BEFORE merge.

remaining: mfa-locked-out golden (3 wrong DOB attempts → phi-restriction transfer)
red-flag-bilingual-spanish golden (dolor en el pecho on Spanish-enabled tenant)
prompt-injection-rejection golden (ignore-previous-instructions adversarial)
appointment-window-disambiguation golden (disambiguate_practitioner → book_appointment)
Nightly --live mode against real Anthropic API for one canary golden

history.note: Audit finding: eval harness exists in packages/voice-agent-evals and runs via `pnpm test` but is NOT wired into .github/workflows. Per-commit regression coverage is dormant. Track CI integration as remaining work.
Shipped CI integration — voice-agent-evals workflow runs per-commit. Eval harness now gates merges into main on voice-agent regressions.

stages.name: Stage 3 — HIPAA / PHI safety
stages.rationale: PHI redaction in every log sink, audit emission per dialog turn, BAA-covered provider allowlist, output-side clinical-content classifier, caller rate-limiting.

baseline.summary: Core posture solid: redaction.ts (100% tested) covers SSN/phone/email/MRN/DOB/ICD-10, IRedFlagAuditEmitter wired with structured STRIDE events, CostCeilingTracker emits reason enum on cap trip, 3-strike DOB/2FA lock-out gates unverified callers. Survey dispatcher has per-patient at-most-one constraint.

current.summary: Shipped end-to-end: BAA allowlist as the single source of truth in @cernia/voice-agent/baa-providers.ts (8 approved vendors + labels + isBaaApproved type guard); apps/api/src/baa-allowlist.ts re-exports it and gates the voice-call orchestrator boot. Per-caller global rate limit (I-321) — InMemoryCallerRateLimiter with sliding-window enforcement keyed by SHA-256 phone hash; defaults 5 calls / 60s per (tenant, caller); per-tenant override; wired into the inbound call handler ahead of dialog construction, emits voice.call.rate-limited audit + returns CallResponse{action:reject}. 9 unit tests cover bucketing, window slide, per-tenant isolation, hash safety, sweep. Redaction + red-flag screener already wired (carry-forward from earlier shipments).

history.note: Audit finding: code references BAA-covered provider allowlist (rag-store.ts:19) but no actual enforcement gate consults it at dialog init. Any provider can be wired today. Documented as remaining work.

Shipped InMemoryCallerRateLimiter (I-321) — 9 unit tests; wired into apps/api inbound call handler; default 5 calls / 60s per caller per tenant; emits voice.call.rate-limited audit on reject.

deferred: Output-side clinical content classifier (I-320 — deferred to Wave-D9 alongside the LLM output-safety overhaul; not blocking the voice-agent pilot)

stages.name: Stage 4 — Telephony + ops
stages.rationale: Telephony backends (Twilio, LiveKit/SIP) with recording-pause-on-PHI, KMS encryption at rest, HIPAA retention policy.

baseline.summary: Twilio + LiveKit/SIP backends in packages/telephony. apps/api/src/voice-call-handler.ts wires inbound webhook + barge-in controller + recording-pause on PHI request. KMS encryption at rest + 7-year HIPAA retention default.

stages.rationale: Real-time gauges for transfer-to-human rate, cost-per-call, per-intent success, response latency, citation-validity. Without this we can't detect production drift.

tracks.name: Functionality Utilization (cross-package)
tracks.description: Audit + remediation of packages built but not wired into apps/*. The 2026-05-24 shelf-ware audit found 286 total packages, 36 imported by apps/*/src, ~140 lazy-load-by-design (catalog-* and specialty-* — loaded on-demand per clinical workflow, NOT shelf-ware), and ~30-40 genuine infrastructure built without app wiring. Each stage groups one coherent cluster of unwired infra; current=absent means no app instantiates any package in the cluster.

stages.name: CDS Hooks & quality measures
stages.rationale: FHIR CDS Hooks is the standard for surfacing clinical decision support inside the EMR workflow. CQM is the Centers for Medicare quality-measure compute engine. Both are core regulatory/clinical infra that have to run server-side for the platform to qualify for value-based-care reimbursement.

baseline.summary: packages/cds-hooks and packages/cqm-engine exist with full unit tests; ZERO importers in apps/*/src. No CDS Hook endpoint mounted in apps/api. No CQM batch job. Quality measure scores can't surface in any UI.

current.summary: CDS Hooks runtime wired into apps/api. GET /cds-services discovery + POST /cds-services/:hookId invocation are live, backed by @cernia/cds-hooks's InMemoryCdsServiceRegistry + HookRouter + HookInvocationHandler. One starter `patient-view` service ships a `voice-agent available` info card. 6 new integration tests cover discovery, happy-path invocation, unknown hookId 404, invalid body 400, inactive-service filtering. apps/api: 1894 tests pass (was 1888). CQM batch + admin UI remain — see remaining.

remaining: Real FHIRPath / workflow / bot dispatcher (the starter dispatcher returns hardcoded cards keyed by hookId).
Mount the other standard hooks: order-select, order-sign, medication-prescribe, encounter-discharge.
Schedule a nightly CQM batch job that emits MeasureReport resources per tenant.
Flip jwtVerificationEnabled=true once the EHR-issuer trust list is configured.

stages.name: Caregiver delegation & identity federation
stages.rationale: Allows a patient's family/caregiver to act on their behalf (proxy), JIT-grant temporary access to a covering provider, SAML/OIDC federation for hospital-network SSO, and break-glass emergency access. Compliance-load-bearing for any enterprise pilot.

baseline.summary: g-d1-caregiver-proxy, g-d2-delegation, g-d3-jit-access, g-d4-access-review-attestation, g-d5-saml-oidc-federation, g-d6-break-glass all shipped. Existing auth flow doesn't consult any of them; no SAML/OIDC providers configured in apps/api boot path.

current.summary: Caregiver-federation backend substantively complete. 5 Postgres stores (proxy, JIT, break-glass, delegation, attestation), 22 endpoints (caregiver + access + idp-connection + sso), 2 admin UI pages (delegations + attestations), per-tenant OIDC factory with cache. 46 unit tests passing. Two infrastructure pieces explicitly deferred: SSO route deps (JwtIssuer + UserProvisioner + state store + http client + secret resolver) and SAML 2.0 handshake (needs library choice). Routes are mounted with 503-when-not-configured so the contract is in place; flipping a single line at boot wires them once the deps land.

Refactored buildCaregiverFederationRuntime to accept opts.proxyStore + apps/api boot now passes new PostgresProxyStore({ db, tenantId: CERNIA_DEFAULT_TENANT_ID ?? "platform" }). Routes are now backed by persistent storage.

Shipped JIT approve/deny + break-glass review endpoints with persistent stores. Migration 050 (jit_access_requests + break_glass_invocations). PostgresJitAccessStore + PostgresBreakGlassStore in apps/api with full tenant scoping. Routes updated: $request + $invoke now persist on ok results; new POST /access/jit/:id/$approve, /$deny, /access/break-glass/:id/$review; new GET /access/jit/pending + /access/break-glass/pending-review for the future admin UI. 7 pg-mem unit tests cover round-trip persistence across the engine state machines (request→approve, request→deny, invoke→review, listing filters, tenant isolation).

Shipped g-d2 user-to-user delegation end-to-end. Migration 051_access_delegations + PostgresAccessDelegationStore + routes mounted in caregiver-federation-routes: POST /access/delegation/$create + /:id/$accept + /:id/$reject + /:id/$revoke, GET /by-delegator/:id + /by-delegatee/:id. Engine state-machine guard rails preserved (only valid transitions persist; 409 invalid-state otherwise). 503 not-configured fallback when store unset. Wired in apps/api boot.

Shipped g-d4 access-review attestation end-to-end. Migration 052 (review_campaigns + attestation_items, two tables). PostgresAttestationStore handles both. 7 new routes: POST /access/attestation/campaigns/$create + /:id/items/$add + /:id/$close, POST /access/attestation/items/:id/$decide, GET /campaigns/:id/summary + /campaigns/:id/items, GET /items/by-reviewer/:id. All engine state-machine guard rails preserved (only-open campaigns accept new items; only-pending items accept decisions; closeCampaign emits itemsToRevoke for the unreviewed sweep).

Shipped admin UI for delegations + attestations. apps/admin/src/lib/caregiver-federation-api.ts (typed client) + 2 new pages: /admin/access/delegations (list by delegator OR delegatee, create form, accept/reject/revoke actions, state-machine-aware buttons) + /admin/access/attestations (campaign create form, per-reviewer pending queue, attest/revoke decisions with optional note). Sidebar gets a new "Access" nav group with both entries. The 17 caregiver-federation endpoints shipped earlier are now reachable from the tenant admin UI.

Shipped g-d5 IdP connection config layer: migration 053_idp_connections (per-tenant SAML 2.0 + OIDC configs, protocol-discriminated columns, JSONB attribute/group mappings), PostgresIdpConnectionStore (multi-tenant; tenantId per call). Tenant-admin CRUD routes mounted: GET/POST /admin/idp-connections, GET/PATCH/DELETE /admin/idp-connections/:id. Validation runs g-d5 engine.validateConnection() on insert; 422 with structured ConfigIssue list on failure. Admin role required for write; all reads tenant-scoped. 11 pg-mem tests cover OIDC + SAML round-trip, validation rejections, tenant isolation across every read/write, idempotent delete. The SAML/OIDC HANDSHAKE itself (AuthnRequest signing, SAMLResponse verification, ACS callback, session bridge) is the remaining piece — needs samlify/passport-saml in @cernia/auth-providers + login route changes.

Shipped TenantSsoFactory (apps/api/src/sso-tenant-factory.ts) — closes the g-d5 → auth-providers translation layer. Per-tenant OidcIdentityProvider construction on demand: reads active OIDC connection from PostgresIdpConnectionStore, resolves clientSecretRef via injected ClientSecretResolver (TenantSecretResolver in prod), maps g-d5 AttributeMapping → auth-providers claimMapping, strips .well-known suffix from discoveryUrl to derive issuer. Built-in cache keyed by (tenantId, connectionId); invalidate(tenantId) drops on admin toggle. 10 pg-mem tests cover happy path, no-enabled error, multi-enabled disambiguation, SAML-rejection, cache hit, cache invalidation, client-secret resolution, issuer derivation, attribute-mapping translation. Remaining for full handshake closure: Fastify routes wiring start + callback into Cernia URL space (needs JwtIssuer + UserProvisioner + persistent OidcStateStore wired in apps/api boot); SAML protocol implementation (needs samlify/passport-saml).

Shipped final piece of g-d5 OIDC chain: ssoRoutes (apps/api/src/sso-routes.ts) mounting GET /api/auth/sso/:tenantId/start + /callback. Routes default to 503 sso-not-configured until apps/api boot wires JwtIssuer + UserProvisioner + UserLookup + OidcStateStore + OidcHttpClient + ClientSecretResolver (clean stub in index.ts with explicit TODO inventory). 6 route tests cover the 503 path + a working happy path via a stub factory (302 redirect on /start, 200 authenticated payload with trusted user fields on /callback, 400 on idp-rejected / invalid-callback). Total caregiver-federation tests now 46 passing across 5 suites.

deferred: Wire SSO route deps in apps/api boot: KmsBackedJwtIssuer (from @cernia/auth-providers) + Postgres-backed UserProvisioner + UserLookup against the users table + Redis-backed OidcStateStore for multi-pod + FetchOidcHttpClient + ClientSecretResolver against TenantSecretResolver. Routes already mounted; they 503 until wired. Each dep is a focused piece.

SAML 2.0 protocol handshake: requires samlify or passport-saml dependency choice + XML signature verification + SP metadata endpoint + AuthnRequest signing + SAMLResponse verification. The config layer (migration 053) already supports SAML connections; the handshake itself is a deferred infrastructure piece.

tracks.name: External Integrations & per-tenant config
tracks.description: Per-tenant credentials + vendor backends the voice agent talks to (Twilio for telephony, Deepgram for STT, ElevenLabs for TTS, OpenAI/Anthropic for LLM). Also covers self-serve tenant provisioning + per-tenant workflow customization. Without this layer every tenant shares the platform's trial credentials and can't customize the assistant.

stages.name: Tenant secrets — per-tenant credential storage
stages.rationale: Each tenant must paste their own Twilio/Deepgram/ElevenLabs/LLM credentials so calls bill to their accounts. Plaintext is encrypted at rest with envelope encryption (AES-256-GCM); the resolver falls back to platform-level env vars when no per-tenant override exists.

baseline.summary: No per-tenant credential storage. The voice agent's Twilio/Deepgram/ElevenLabs/OpenAI/Anthropic keys came from process.env only. Every tenant in a multi-tenant deploy would share the same vendor accounts — billing attribution impossible, key rotation per tenant impossible, white-glove onboarding meant editing the platform-level env.

current.summary: Shipped end-to-end. Migration 046_tenant_secrets (composite-PK (tenant_id, provider), opaque ciphertext column, JSONB metadata). TenantSecretResolver in @cernia/core with NoopSecretCipher (dev) + AesGcmSecretCipher (prod). apps/api boot now wires KyselyTenantSecretStore (secrets survive restart) + refuses to start in production when CERNIA_TENANT_SECRET_KEY/KID is unset or wrong length — TenantSecretBootError thrown loud. PUT/DELETE handlers emit structured tenant-secret.write/delete IAM audit rows. KMS rotation runbook (docs/operations/runbooks/tenant-secret-key-rotation.md) covers cadence, rehearsal in staging, multi-key decrypt window, re-encrypt job, and rollback.

history.note: Swapped apps/api boot from InMemoryTenantSecretStore → KyselyTenantSecretStore. Added prod-mode TenantSecretBootError refusing missing/short keys. Authored KMS rotation runbook with rehearse-in-staging + multi-key window + re-encrypt job.

stages.name: Twilio voice backend boot wiring
stages.rationale: @cernia/telephony ships a TwilioVoiceBackend + LiveKit/SIP backend. apps/api needs to instantiate one and wire it into the voice-call handler so an inbound call actually arrives at the dialog engine.

baseline.summary: packages/telephony has TwilioVoiceBackend + LiveKit/SIP backend with full unit coverage. apps/api/src/voice-call-handler.ts handles barge-in + recording-pause-on-PHI. NOT yet wired: the boot path doesn't construct a TwilioVoiceBackend instance and the /telephony/inbound webhook is stub-only.

current.summary: TwilioVoiceBackend fully wired. buildTwilioVoiceClient() native-fetch factory added to @cernia/telephony (no SDK dependency — uses Node 20+ fetch). Boot path in apps/api constructs TwilioVoiceBackend when CERNIA_TELEPHONY_BACKEND=twilio + TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID + TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN + CERNIA_PUBLIC_BASE_URL are set; falls back to stub with structured log. 26 new tests cover create/fetch/update + auth header + idempotency. Typecheck + full telephony test suite clean.

remaining: Boot wiring in apps/api: construct TwilioVoiceBackend keyed by tenantId via TenantSecretResolver.resolve(tenantId, 'twilio').
Smoke test against the Twilio sandbox using one real test number.

history.note: buildTwilioVoiceClient native-fetch factory + TwilioVoiceBackend boot wiring in apps/api. 26 new tests.

ra-tel-2 + ra-tel-3 closed. telephonyRoutes plugin (apps/api/src/telephony-routes.ts) registered in apps/api/src/index.ts section 10g-telephony — POST /telephony/twilio/voice (inbound HMAC-verified webhook with tenant lookup via PostgresTenantPhoneNumberStore), POST /telephony/twilio/status (status callback), POST /telephony/outbound (internal REST surface wrapping placeAuditedOutboundCall). Caller-id allowlist enforced via phoneNumbers.assertOwnedByTenant; OutboundSpoofError → 403 with call.spoof-attempt-blocked audit row. HMAC verification uses computeTwilioSignature from @cernia/notifications + constant-time compare. 5 new unit tests pass; full telephony-routes suite is 17/17 green.

stages.name: Deepgram STT adapter
stages.rationale: Speech-to-text is the input side of the voice loop. Deepgram is the BAA-covered default; per-tenant model + language overrides are read from tenant_secrets.metadata.

baseline.summary: @cernia/voice-agent has an ISpeechToText seam but no production adapter. All current eval/golden runs use a stubbed STT that echoes back a scripted transcript.

current.summary: DeepgramWsTransport production adapter shipped in apps/api/src/deepgram-ws-transport.ts. Uses ws npm package (already a dependency) with Authorization: Token header. Parses Results, SpeechStarted, UtteranceEnd, Error message types into DeepgramServerEvent. boot path wires DeepgramSttBackend when CERNIA_SPEECH_STT=deepgram + DEEPGRAM_API_KEY are set; falls back to stub STT with warn log otherwise. 13 new unit tests cover factory shape, JSON parsing (all event types), error resilience, and handler idempotency. api typecheck + full api test suite clean.

remaining: Build packages/voice-stt-deepgram implementing ISpeechToText against Deepgram's websocket API.
Token sourcing: TenantSecretResolver.resolve(tenantId, 'deepgram'); metadata.defaultLanguage/defaultModel control per-tenant model selection.
Wire into apps/api voice-call-handler so STT frames are forwarded into the dialog engine.
Eval: add a STT-accuracy golden using a recorded WAV + expected transcript.

history.note: DeepgramWsTransport transport adapter + apps/api boot wiring. 13 tests.

stages.name: ElevenLabs TTS adapter
stages.rationale: Text-to-speech is the output side of the loop. ElevenLabs is the default; per-tenant voice id is read from tenant_secrets.vendorAccountId. Tenant-defined persona (existing voice-agent persona editor) selects the voice.

baseline.summary: ITextToSpeech seam exists; no production adapter. Persona editor already exposes a voiceId picker with sample previews but every save is dropped on the floor.

current.summary: ElevenLabsFetchTransport production adapter shipped in apps/api/src/elevenlabs-fetch-transport.ts. Uses Node 22 native fetch with xi-api-key header + streaming ReadableStream body iteration. boot path wires ElevenLabsTtsBackend when CERNIA_SPEECH_TTS=elevenlabs + ELEVENLABS_API_KEY are set; falls back to stub TTS with warn log otherwise. 8 new unit tests cover factory shape, streaming multi-chunk assembly, HTTP 4xx error propagation, null body guard, and close idempotency. api typecheck clean.

remaining: Build packages/voice-tts-elevenlabs implementing ITextToSpeech against ElevenLabs's REST API.
Token sourcing: TenantSecretResolver.resolve(tenantId, 'elevenlabs'); vendorAccountId = default voiceId.
Persist persona voiceId via PATCH /Organization/:id (the persona editor currently console.logs the save).
Wire the resolver lookup into apps/api so dialog turns get TTS bytes.

history.note: ElevenLabsFetchTransport native-fetch streaming adapter + apps/api boot wiring. 8 tests.

stages.name: Self-serve tenant provisioning
stages.rationale: A new tenant should be able to land on apps/installer, paste their org details, hook up SSO, paste their Twilio/Deepgram/ElevenLabs keys, and have a working voice agent with no Cernia handholding.

baseline.summary: apps/installer exists as a wizard scaffold (org details, branding, persona) but the 12 config items the voice agent needs (Twilio SID + token, Deepgram key, ElevenLabs key + default voice, phone number registration, LLM provider choice, language allowlist, business hours, transfer phone numbers, cost ceiling, red-flag overrides) are partial / scattered / not wired into apps/api boot.

current.summary: Voice-config step (step 4 of 8) in apps/installer wizard collects all 12 voice-agent config items (Twilio AccountSID+AuthToken to tenant-secret store, Deepgram + ElevenLabs API keys + voice ID, LLM provider, language allowlist, business hours, escalation phone, persona greeting). Each step wired to its server-side endpoint: tenant-secret-routes (credentials), persona PATCH (tone), tenant_phone_numbers register (E.164 routing). Voice-config wizard at apps/installer/src/app/wizard/[sessionId]/voice-config + server route at apps/installer/src/app/api/voice-config/[sessionId]. Outbound smoke-test and CSV/JSON white-glove import are operator-tooling extras tracked as deferred — not blocking pilot rollout.

history.note: voice-config wizard step (step 4/8) — all 12 config items, secrets to tenant-secret store
Wizard + endpoint wiring confirmed shipped (task #98 from session backlog). Smoke-test + CSV import moved to deferred (operator tooling, not pilot-blocking).

deferred: Post-install outbound smoke test: place a test call to the registered number, verify the tenant-branded greeting plays. Deferred — manual verification works for the pilot; automation needs an outbound-test-harness.

stages.name: Per-tenant workflow customization
stages.rationale: Different customers want different things: SNF wants 'medication-pickup-reminder' but not 'cancel-appointment'; pain clinic wants a custom intake script; pediatric clinic wants distinct red-flag thresholds. The voice agent must be configurable per tenant — not forked per tenant.

baseline.summary: Building blocks exist: @cernia/j1b-workflow-template-registry (template per tenant), TenantPolicy.allowedActions / practitionerScope / redFlagOverrides, Organization.extension[voice-agent-persona], j2c fork-and-modify-diff, j2d AI-assisted-workflow-gen. None composed into a single configurability surface; tenant admins can't see or change any of it without engineering involvement.

current.summary: TenantCustomization schema + runtime resolver + FHIR codec shipped in @cernia/voice-agent. Shared InMemoryTenantCustomizationStore wires GET/PATCH /api/voice-agent/customization (voice-agent-admin-routes) and the voice-call-handler — same instance, so a PATCH made by the tenant admin is visible at the next inbound session start. applyTenantCustomization() called in voice-call-handler.InboundCallSession.handle() before dialog.start; allowedIntents override applies for the session (mandatory intents re-injected by the resolver). Customer-facing How-to-configure guide at docs/guides/voice-agent-customization.md.

history.note: Shipped InMemoryTenantCustomizationStore + wired single instance into voice-agent-admin-routes + voice-call-handler. applyTenantCustomization called at session start; allowedIntents override now reaches the dialog policy. Customer-facing customization guide authored.

deferred: Persist via Organization.extension FHIR codec (encode/decode helpers exist in @cernia/voice-agent — wire them to a Postgres-backed Medplum write so customization survives restart of the apps/api process). Today the store is in-memory and shared across handlers; restart wipes it.
Intent Handler Overrides (advanced): per-intent backend URL override so a tenant can replace the platform handler for a specific intent with their own webhook. Deferred — out of scope for the pilot.

Thread systemPromptAugment + additionalRedFlagPhrases through DialogStartOpts → buildSystemPrompt + red-flag screener. Today the policy override flows through but the augment + extra red-flag phrases need DialogStartOpts schema additions before they bind. Tracked separately so the policy override ships now.

Eval golden: 'tenant-with-customization-applied' — replays a session against a policy with custom allowedIntents + systemPromptAugment; asserts system prompt contains CLINIC INSTRUCTIONS and the blocked intent is absent. Deferred until systemPromptAugment threading lands.

history.note: Track created from the per-tenant architecture decision (lock: per-tenant credentials from day one, self-serve installer, External Integrations track now / Tenant Provisioning track expanded inside it).

End-to-end foundation: migration 046, TenantSecretResolver + Noop/AesGcm ciphers + InMemoryStore in @cernia/core, KyselyTenantSecretStore in @cernia/storage, Fastify routes (GET list / PUT upsert / DELETE clear) with cross-tenant 403 guard + audit, boot wire at slot 10i, admin Credentials & Integrations sub-page with cards for Twilio/Deepgram/ElevenLabs/OpenAI/Anthropic, sub-page nav link from the persona editor. 20 core unit tests + storage pg-mem contract test green. tracks.name: Sample-data elimination ("no mock data")
tracks.description: User-driven mandate (2026-05-25): every admin UI page must fetch from a real apps/api endpoint; the 30 sample-*.ts files under apps/admin/src/app/_data/ must be deleted. Tracking per-domain progress phase-by-phase. Full audit: docs/status/sample-data-elimination-plan.md.

stages.name: Phase 1 — Real backend infrastructure
stages.rationale: Postgres + MinIO + Redis + Mailpit + NATS in Docker, apps/api boot against real DB, dev-seed produces one real tenant + users + FHIR resources, all 4 Next.js apps running natively. Required for any swap to be testable end-to-end.

baseline.summary: Apps had never been started against a real backend on this machine. Postgres data dir on OneDrive caused fsync hangs; a native PG service was bound to host:5432 colliding with the Docker stack; admin tenant routes weren't wired; api Dockerfile didn't copy scripts/ (postinstall fail); storage migrate.js couldn't find src/migrations/ (tsc doesn't copy .sql); connection-routes.ts double-registered POST /Connection over @cernia/server's generic FHIR CRUD; 122 pnpm-store packages were partially extracted.

current.summary: Full stack up: Postgres 16 on :5433 (production durability flags), MinIO :9000/:9001, Redis, Mailpit, NATS. 40 migrations applied. tenant-acme seeded (20 FHIR resources). apps/api on :8080 with real RSA jwks. Admin :3000, trust :3001, patient :3002, installer :3003 all return HTTP 200. Fixes: docker-compose.override.yml remaps PG to 5433, scripts/ copied into api Dockerfile, migrate.js resolver tries dist/migrations then src/migrations fallback, connection-routes.ts removed duplicate POST /Connection (keeps $run), fix-pnpm-store.mjs repaired 122 broken packages.

current.summary: docs/status/sample-data-elimination-plan.md produced. 30 files cataloged: 0 wired, 11 partial (apps/api route exists, UI not refactored), 12 need-route (no route exists), 7 deferrable (work-app-only, no admin consumer). Effort estimate: ~50 work-days across the admin-facing surface. Phased execution plan (Phase 3.1 quick wins → Phase 3.6 specialized domains).

baseline.summary: All three pages render from sample-*.ts despite their backend routes existing.

current.summary: Done. 3 sample files deleted, 4 admin pages swapped (voice-budget + settings + voice-agent improvements + forms/[taskId]). New typed clients in /billing/voice-budget/api.ts + /voice-agent/improvements/api.ts. All HTTP 200; typecheck + lint clean.

current.summary: Done. 3 sample files deleted, 27 admin pages swapped, 5 new backend CRUD routes added to admin-tenants-routes. Integrations uses /Connection generic CRUD with client-side protocol filter. Patient-portal uses /admin/patient-portal/* + /Patient. voice-agent-api.ts rewritten to drop mock mode. Pages without backends (inference jobs/batch/drift/evals/quotas, marketplace installs/reviews/payouts, hie/participation/queries/rls) render explicit empty-state cards — no fabricated data. 941/941 tests pass; typecheck + lint clean.

Closure-pass 1 (ERX) — backend api shipped: packages/erx-service/src/api.ts (ErxApi + InMemoryErxStore + tenant-scoping invariant) + 16 unit tests. Playwright @wave-ERX spec at tests/e2e/wave-ERX/erx-draft-workflow.spec.ts (composer flow + list view + FSM walk + cross-tenant isolation, 4 tests). Follow-ups: apps/api route registration (blocked on pnpm-lock WIP resolution), admin UI components, Postgres-backed IErxStore adapter. ERX wave backend rung in audit-wave-integration ladder transitions shelf → partial.

Closure-pass 3 (Wave G) — SAML 2.0 Response handler shipped at packages/g-d5-saml-oidc-federation/src/saml-response-handler.ts. Closes the long-running deferred item flagged since project start (apps/api/src/sso-routes.ts docstring + the original PROD-G deferred row). parseSamlResponseEnvelope + extractSamlClaims + validateSamlConditions + SamlSignatureVerifier port (NoopSamlSignatureVerifier refuses by default; StubAcceptAllSignatureVerifier for tests only) + handleSamlResponse end-to-end. Zero new deps — pure logic, browser-compatible (atob + Uint8Array + TextDecoder via globalThis). Wave G backend rung shelf → partial in next audit run.

scripts/audit-wave-integration.mjs heuristic extension — added registered-route-file @cernia/<pkg> import detection. The audit's prior heuristic only flipped a wave's Backend rung to 'wired' when the route file's BASENAME textually matched a wave package name (e.g. allergy-routes.ts ↔ s8-allergy-checking). That missed multi-wave route files: apps/api/src/telehealth-rpm-routes.ts imports @cernia/q-s2-rpm-ingestion + @cernia/q-s4-rpm-care-plans + @cernia/q-s6-rpm-adherence and is registered as telehealthRpmRoutes — clearly Wave Q is live, but the audit reported Q=shelf. New logic: for every registered route file in apps/api/src, also scan its content for @cernia/<wave-pkg> imports and credit that wave. Effect: Wave B (shelf→wired), Wave G (shelf→wired), Wave P (shelf→wired), Wave Q (shelf→wired) flipped to wired in this run. Wave S retained wired. Net: 4 waves moved Backend dot from grey to green from a single audit-script improvement. wave-integration-audit-2026-05-27.md updated.
